name: Jim Pugh
abstract: James Edward Pugh (born November 12, 1950) is an American trombonist and composer. He was a trombonist with Woody Herman (1972–1976) and briefly with Chick Corea before concentrating on session work.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: Trombones: well myself and Jim Pugh, Dave Taylor, Barry Rogers, the guys that played in the group Dreams with the Brecker brothers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: He made two entrances into our band. The first time he came as a second trombonist to Bobby Burgess

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: Then Jim Pugh and Dave Taylor called me up, “hey I just heard what you did for John Marcellus. Why don’t you do something for us?” And so I wound up with all this
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: we were like the new group of guys. It was kind of interesting, some of those sessions. ... Ronnie Cubers, Jim Pugh, Dave Taylors
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: Jim Pugh and Birch Johnson and all these guys that I really looked up to and still do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024
